source_anchors.what_text_says: The material frames filesystem state as durable memory for complex tasks.
The method applies to complex tasks and skips simple ones.

source_anchors.source_ref: Quick Start, File Purposes, Critical Rules
source_anchors.what_text_says: The source separates plan, findings, and progress and tells when to update each.
Errors should be logged and repeated failures should change strategy or escalate.
The source contains host-specific runtime enforcement details.
does_not_support: ReaderLab should adopt Claude plugin shell as default

baseline_summary_trap: This Skill tells agents to create task_plan.md, findings.md, and progress.md before doing complex work.
upgrade_question: How can a complex AI-assisted workflow remain recoverable across context loss, task drift, and repeated failures without turning every small task into process overhead?

lens_candidates.clarifies: why the method writes state before the agent forgets
lens_candidates.boundary_power: separates durable state from chat memory
lens_candidates.reader_gain: reader can transfer the idea to ReaderLab annotation continuity

lens_candidates.text_trigger: skip list for simple questions and quick lookups
lens_candidates.clarifies: why the method needs task-size boundaries
lens_candidates.reader_gain: reader can decide when not to use it

lens_candidates.lens: Claude hook architecture as ReaderLab default

absorb: Use error records to change strategy.
downgrade: Exact filenames and save frequency should be adapted to ReaderLab rather than copied literally.
reject: Do not adopt Claude plugin hook protocol as ReaderLab architecture.

reader_facing_explanation.title: 这不是计划模板，而是把复杂任务变成可恢复系统
reader_facing_explanation.body: The reader-facing page explains the Skill as recoverability design and separates runtime shell from transferable method.

delta_eval.reader_gain: Reader can distinguish persistent work-memory design from template or hook copying.
